idea: Build RoomReady, a meeting-room readiness console for a fictional enterprise campus. Facilities coordinators need to see rooms, equipment checks, current readiness, and the exact blocker preventing a room from being usable.
audience: Corporate facilities coordinators responsible for meeting-room readiness
outcome: Blocked rooms can be found and restored quickly, with global readiness totals updating immediately when a failed equipment check is resolved.
constraints: Run entirely locally with no backend, authentication, database, analytics, external APIs, or runtime network calls
Use fictional deterministic seed data and provide a visible reset action
Be keyboard operable with visible focus and never communicate state by color alone
Keep the primary narrated demo under three minutes
must_have_capabilities: Show total, ready, and blocked room counts derived from seeded room and checklist state
Filter rooms by floor and readiness
Inspect a room's equipment checklist and identify its exact blocker
Resolve one failed checklist item and update both the room state and global totals
Reset all demo data to its deterministic initial state
visual_direction: A calm, polished enterprise facilities console with excellent hierarchy, generous spacing, accessible status labels, and clear before-and-after feedback.
demo_preferences: Start with the full readiness overview, filter to blocked rooms, open one room, resolve its failed equipment check, visibly show the room and global totals changing, then reset the app.
